Prophylactic use of acyclovir in the third trimester should be considered in women with frequent HSV infection outbreaks.
Acyclovir should be given to all pregnant women with active genital HSV infection near term or at the time of delivery.
All women presenting in labor should be asked about symptoms of HSV infections.
All laboring women with active genital HSV infections should be delivered by     cesarean section.
Herpes simplex infection of the newborn

path: Infection / Infection / 具抗藥性細菌的感染 (Part 5)
content:
General considerations: Herpes simplex virus infection results from perinatal exposure to the virus.
Cesarean section is indicated if the mother has signs of active infection or if she has virologic culture evidence of herpes and the membranes are ruptured < 4-6 hours.
Primary HSV infection late in pregnancy poses a higher risk of transmission to the infant than do primary infection occurring before or early in pregnancy.
Clinical presentations
Diagnosis
Viral culture from skin lesions, conjunctiva, throat, feces/urine, nasopharynx, 
and CSF.
Tzanck smear
Serologic test : less useful
Lumbar puncture should be performed in all suspected cases. And evidence of  hemorrhagic CNS infection with increased WBC, RBC and protein is found.
CSF for PCR
CT or MRI to demonstrate the extent and severity of the brain injury.
Treatment
Isolation
Acyclovir 60 mg/kg/day divided q8h for 14-21 days
SEM disease(Skin, Eyes, Mucosal membranes/Mouth) : 14 days ;
Disseminated or CNS disease : 21 days

Pathogenesis
Pathogen: Varicella zoster virus (VZV) : DNA virus, no other natural host except human
Spread: (highly communicable : 90 % of household)
Airborne droplet
Direct contact with skin lesion
Transplacental passage
Incidence of chickenpox in pregnancy : 0.05 ~ 0.8 %
Pathogenesis of VZV infection : 
Nasopharynx => viral replication : 4~7 days in tonsils, regional LN, salivary glands => 
Primary viremia => other organ => secondary viremia => invasion of cutaneous tissue 
=> Characteristic exanthem ( vesicle : 3 ~ 7 days )
Infectious period(可傳染期): 48 hrs before rash ~ crusting rash
No new lesion for 3 days
Perinatal chickenpox:
Acquired postnatally by droplet infection
Trans-placentally transmitted : occur within 10 d/o
Incubation : 10 ~ 21 days
After VZIG :↑incubation to 28 days
Immunocompromised patient : ↓incubation
Usual interval from onset of rash of mother to onset in the neonate : 9 ~ 15 days.
Varicella can develop between 1-16 d/o in infants born to mothers with active varicella
Infection of neonate : 10 ~ 20 % if mother have acute varicella within the periods from 5 days before to 2 days after delivery (即所謂的前五後二規則，在此期間母親尚未產生抗體，無法保護新生兒，infection/ mortality rate高)
Mortality ( before acyclovir ) : 20 ~ 30 %
Most common life threatening complication : pneumonia
Management of mother
VZIG : not recommend and not effective once disease is established
Oral acyclovir :not recommend routinely for pregnant woman with uncomplicated varicella
IV acyclovir : for pregnant woman with serious complication
Safety of acyclovir during pregnancy ?
Duration of infant in utero ↑⇒maternal Ab transplacental transfer↑
Early delivery of the infant of mother with active varicella: prohibited 
(絕對禁止提早產出，除非有fetal distress)
Management of infant
VZIG : (Ref : Avery’s Diseases of the Newborn 9th edition)
Soon after exposure ( < 72~ 96 hr/o )
For NB if mother has acute varicella within the periods from 5 days before to 2 days after delivery.
For premature if mother with active chickenpox ( even> 1 wk )
Not indicated if mother has zoster
Dose : 125 units/10kg im, max : 625 units ( protection : 3 wks )
Taiwan: no VZIG available, can use IVIG 400mg/kg
VZIG prophylaxis : 1/2 infants ⇒neonatal chickenpox , but mild.
IV acyclovir
Dose: 15mg/kg/dose Q8H if s/s severe
Duration : 7~10 days or until no new lesions have appeared for 48 hrs
Isolation
Airborne & contact precaution (空氣及接觸隔離)
Minimum of 5 days after the onset of rash & as long at the rash remains vesicular
Neonate born to mother with varicella: continued until 21 d/o 
or continued until 28 d/o if receive VZIG
Breastfeeding is not contraindicated.

path: Infection / Infection / 疑似愛滋病嬰幼兒檢驗項目
content:
Child: HIV Ab (ELISA，院內開單)(+) ->聯絡感染管制(分機3091) 外送CDC 作Western blot confirmation
Newborn: NAT (HIV分子生物學核酸檢測 Nucleic Acid Testing)，應24小時內通報並經感控送檢體至CDC
CBC,DC, RBC.,PLT, CRP, B/C, RPR, (TPHA, HCV Ab, TORCH titer prn, depending on maternal history)
口服：6-12hr內使用Zidovudine 2mg/kg q6H ( 10mg/cc) *6 wks for prophylaxis，
需專案申請
之後於1-2,4 m/o follow HIV NAT
預防愛滋病垂直傳染用藥 (2023更新，Ref: 台灣愛滋病學會: 愛滋病檢驗及治療指引第六版「第六章、避免愛滋病毒母子垂直感染之治療建議」)

Frequency: 
		In most NICU, the top 3 causes of nosocomial infection.
Incidence: VLBW: 2.2-12.9 %    ELBW: 5.5-16.5 %
Risk factors of neonatal candidiasis
Altered colonization of mucosal surfaces:
Exposure to broad spectrum antibiotics
Delayed enteral feeds
Prolonged parenteral nutrition
H2 receptor antagonists
Topical petrolatum (vaseline凡士林) ointment
Facilitating the invasion:
Prematurity
Major congenital malformations
Indwelling catheters and tubes
Surgery
Postnatal steroids
Systemic infections at birth and in 1st 24 hr of life:
Pathogenesis:
From trans-placental hematogenous spread
From amniotic fluid infection, caused by ascent of organism from densely colonized or symptomatically infected birth canal
Fortunately, remain superficial
However, systemic spread and deep inoculation by invasive procedures that breach involved areas.
Implicated species:
Candida albicans: most common
Others: Candida tropicalis,C.parapsilosis, C. lusitaniae, C.glabrata, C. krusei, and C. guilliermondii.
Fungal balls:
Particularly associated with C .albicans and C. tropicalis
Resulted in urethral obstruction, acute renal failure and hydronephrosis
Candida colonization
Definition: the non-pathogenic co-existence of microorganism with the host.
Species:
Candida albicans: are also most predominant in all categories
Candida glabrata: most prominent commensal and pathogen among the total high risk population
Candida parapsilosis: predilection for the premature NB infants.
Candida colonization is usually found at human skin, GI tract, female genitourinary tract.
Vertical and horizontal transmission
Vertical transmission:
From maternal GI tract or GU tract during perinatal period.
C. albicans : 33 % of premature infants.
Important risk factors: increased density of maternal colonization or frank maternal vaginitis.
Presentations:
Term infants: oral thrush or perineal dermatitis (systemic disease rarely occur)
Prematurity: local or disseminated disease (if have additional risk factors)
Horizontal transmission:
From care providers
C. parapsilosis is most common
Epidemiology and risk factors: (targets for prevention)
2 common causes: C. albicans, C. parapsilosis
Incidence in ELBW: 4 to 15 %
Risk factor
Thrombocytopenia
Central vascular access
Lower birth weight
Antibiotics use, esp. 3rd generation cephalosporin exposure
Lower GA
Mechanical ventilation
Feeding intolerance
Potential target to reduce incidence:
Aggressive use of CPAP rather than IMV
Employment of peripheral rather than central vascular access whenever possible
Emphasis on enteral feeding
Avoiding systemic steroid
Minimizing antibiotics exposure, and suggest use of more narrow-spectrum antibiotics.
Consequences of candidemia (End organ damage)
All tissues including neonatal retina, brain, heart, lung, liver, spleen and joints.
Management:
If with proven candidemia : lumbar puncture, brain and abdomen ultrasound, ophthalmologic examination and echocardiogram
If positive gram stain for Candida from blood:

Urgently (within 24 hr) remove or replace all central vascular catheters
Replacement is defined as the removal of an infected catheter from one location, and replacement of a catheter in a different location.
Removal of catheter without immediate replacement would be ideal 
(但若不得已而必須要馬上插回時，請更換位置)
With positive culture, the antifungal therapy should start immediately.

path: Infection / Infection / Candida Renal disease and UTI in neonates
content:
Pathogen: Candida spp. is a common cause of UTI in NICU.
Range: from isolated candiduria to obstructive fungus balls.
Diagnosis:
UTI:
Sterile urethral catheterization (導尿) > 104 CFU/ml
Suprapubic aspiration > 103 CFU/ml
Diagnosis of renal candidiasis by ultrasound:
Fungal ball
Renal parenchymal infiltration
Medical management:
Amphotericin-B as drug of choice
Dosage : 1 mg/kg/day qd, drip 6 hours (Dr.許drip 12 hours, 且要記得monitor K & Mg) (用D5W, or D10W稀釋10倍, ex: 1mg in 10ml) for at least 2 wks after consecutive negative blood and urine cultures.
If serum Creatinine > 1.5mg/dl or rising > 0.3mg/dl/day, 改q48 hr or 增加drip duration (如drip 8-10 hrs)
If acute renal failure or candidal pyelonephritis→改用lipid formulation of amphotericin-B.
Fluconazole:
Added to antifungal therapy if persistent positive culture or with amphotericin B interval q48h(不建議單獨使用)
Less effective against no-albicans spp. compared to Amphotericin-B.
70 % excreted unchanged in urine→use in isolated Candida UTI especially with C. albicans.
Bladder instillation (若systemic Ampho-B效果不好，可考慮使用，非routine)
5-15 mg of Ampho-B dissolved in 100 ml of sterile water
Filled the bladder to near capacity dwell for 60-120 minutes
3-4x/day until clinical resolution (typically 2-5 days)
Prognosis:
Isolated candidiuria: rarely fatal, but fatality was associated with disseminated disease.
Mucocutaneous manifestations of candidiasis
註: 若為ELBW infants, 且合併有oral thrush or diaper dermatitis (with/without GI anomaly, stomy等), or other disseminated skin lesion (like fungus infection appearance)
必須注意已有fungus colonization, 甚至因為ELBW infants with poor skin barrier, 易侵犯至血液, 造成invasive fungemia.
必須儘快作fungus study [包括urine routine (注意有無yeast), B/C, U/C, CSF/C, (含fungus culture), 以及CBC/DC, PLT(有無leukocytosis, thrombocytopenia), CRP, BUN, Cr, GOT, GPT, electrolyte (尤其K/Mg)]並儘快給予systemically treatment. (Amphotericin-B).

Classic presentation:
Diffuse cutaneous disease presents at birth or within the first day of life.
Extensive erythematous papules and plaque that evolve to pustules (bullae) and vesicles then desquamation.
Palms and soles frequently involved
Burn-like dermatitis in VLBW infants
Risk factors:
Uterine foreign body (如子宮避孕器)
Intrauterine transmission (Hematogenous, PROM)
Treatment:
Term baby: topical antifungal therapy alone (Canesten), except clinical presentation suggests sepsis.
Prematurity: systemic treatment (esp. GA < 27 wks or ELBW)
Antifungal drugs
Amphotericin B, the drug of choice for systemic antifungal therapy.
Poor water solubility, and poorly absorbed, except intravenous form
Most species of Candida are considered susceptible to Amphotericin-B, but resistance develops in C. krusei and C. glabrata)
Dosage：
1 mg/kg/day QD; dilute with D5W or D10W (含電解質會沉澱) 10倍 
(Max <0.2mg/ml), ex: 1mg→10ml
Drip 6 hrs (Dr.許drip 12 hrs, 稀釋與慢速可以降低infusion reaction)
Fungemia only: 等B/C (於治療後1 wk做) sterile後再2 wks(即至少3 wks)
Total accumulative dosage ranges : 25–35 mg/kg over 6 weeks or longer
Fungemia with meningitis : B/C sterile後再4 wks (即至少5 wks)
(但至少停藥前要確認CSF/C sterile)
Monitor K, Mg after 12 hours later (VS許) CBC, GOT, BUN, Cr, serum electrolytes every week, discontinue if BUN > 40 mg/dL, Cr > 3 mg/dL
Therapeutic drug monitor
Peak level (20-30 mcg/mL) – 30min after IV dose
Trough level (2-5 mcg/mL) – 30 min before the next dose
Toxicity:
Acute toxicity: fever, rigors and chills [in adults, but neonate (-)]
Nephrotoxicity / ototoxciity:
In adult : permanent renal insufficiency or renal failure, oliguria, hypokalemia, azotemia, and hyponatremia.
In neonate : mild nephrotoxicity that resolved after cessation of therapy.
併用其他 nephrotoxic / ototoxic medications (lasix, vancomycin) may increase adverse effects, allergy (rash, fever), thrombophlebitis, neutropenia (>3 wks)
Lipid formulation: (ex. Liposomal amphotericin-B: AmBisone)
Advantage: deliver a higher dose with less toxicity.
Improved CNS penetration
CSF concentration with conventional Ampho-B reach 40-90 % of serum level.
Used for patients with preexisting renal insufficiency or benefit from increase doses. (conventional Tx. failed)
Diflucan (Fluconazole 100mg/50ml/vial)
Dosage of fluconazole is the same for oral and IV administration
無culture proof而高度懷疑fungal infection使用treatment dose
culture確定有fungal infection則改用Amphotericin-B or Ambisome
Premature neonates:
Treatment dose: loading: 12~25mg/kg/dose, maintain: 12mg/kg/dose
≦GA29wk：Postnatal age 0-14 days: q48h；> 14days: q24h
＞GA30wk：Postnatal age 0-7 days: q48h；> 7days: q24h
References: Pappas’ et al, Treatment Guidelines for Candidiasis, CID 2009:48 (1 March) , pp. 503-535,
Manual of neonatal care, 7 th edition, p.650
Pediatric dosage handbook with international trade names index, 16th edition, p.532-533
NEOFAX 2011, p42
Neonates > 14 days, infants, children: once daily: see table
Dosing adjustment in renal impairment:

CCr<50: administer 50% of recommended dose
Hemodialysis: one recommended dose after each dialysis
Administration
Oral: administer with or without food; shake suspension well before use
Parenteral: IVD over 2hr, Max. rate 200mg/hr, concentration 2mg/ml, 以N/S、D20W、Ringer’s solution、含KCl的葡萄糖液、4.2%NaHCO3稀釋，以10ml/min速度做靜脈輸注。不可IM/IVP
Fluconazole prophylaxis policy (2013年修訂)
Indication: High risk for fungal infection: 
GA≦27+6 weeks or BW ≦1000g (ELBW),
less than 5 days old, therapy≧2 antibiotics, 3rd generation cephalosporins, H2 antagonist, postnatal steroids, Facilitate fungal growth (TPN > 5 days, intravenous lipid), congenital cutaneous candidiasis, uterine foreign body (如子宮避孕器), intrauterine transmission (Hematogenous, PROM, maternal vaginal culture: fungus), requires central vascular access, without liver failure.
本院新生兒加護病房以high risk premature baby為targeted fluconazole prophylaxis之主要對象，因此必須individualized；是否給prophylaxis可先與主治大夫討論後再給藥(if no definite risk factors)
Dosage：3mg/kg/dose，Twice dose weekly (QW25，健保，不共用) IVD 60 mins
(2021增修) 劑量可無條件進位至整數，因UpToDate and Micromedex 建議劑量為
3 to 6 mg/kg/dose, twice weekly
Duration:
Since admission to our NICU; duration up to 6 weeks.
或者當baby臨床狀況穩定，毫無fungal infection之跡象，且已達full feeding(完全不再需要任何IV line)時，可經主治大夫決定後，視情況提早DC Diflucan prophylaxis.
References:
1. C. Mary Healy, et al. Pediatrics 2008;121;703-710              2. Smart Uko, et al, Pediatrics 2006;117;1243-1252
3. J-H Weitkamp , te al, Journal of Perinatology (2008), 1–7  4. David A. Kaufman, Current Opinion in Pediatrics 2008, 20:332-320.                                                                   5. Manual of neonatal care, 6th edition, p.691

path: Infection / Infection / Antibiotics and Anti-Fungal agent 劑量 (Part 1)
content:
Meropenem (500mg/vial)
Neonates (Dose 請參考NICU routine-抗生素劑量章節)
Children ≧3 mo: 60mg/k/day div q8h; meningitis: 120mg/k/day div q8h; Max:6g/day
Dosage adjustment in renal impairment:
CCr 26-50: standard dose q12h
CCr 10-25: one-half dose q12h
CCr <10: one-half dose q24h
Monitoring Parameters: Periodic renal, hepatic, and hematologic function tests
Administration: IV push or IV intermittent infusion; final concentration for administration should not exceed 50mg/ml; infuse IV push injection over 3-5min; intermittent infusion dose should be administered over 15-30mins.
Metronidazole (500mg/100ml BT)
Neonates: anaerobic infections  (Dose 請參考NICU routine-抗生素劑量章節)
Infants and children:
Amebiasis: oral: 35-50mg/k/day div q8h
Other parasitic infections: oral: 15-30mg/k/day div q8h
Anaerobic infections: oral, IV: 30mg/k/day div q6h; Max:4g/day
Dosing adjustment in hepatic impairment: 50%~67% decrease in dosage
Administration:
Oral: administer on an empty stomach; may administer with food if GI upset occurs
Parenteral: IV slow intermittent infusion over 30-60 min at a final concentration for administration of 5-8mg/ml
# IVP, IM:不可,  IV infusion: 可
Imipenem/Cilastatin (Tienam)：
Dosage : 20-25mg/kg/dose q12h IV drip >30min (D5W or D10W or N/S)
Adverse effects : seizures, local reactions at injection site, increased platelet counts, eosinophilia, elevated hepatic transaminases, diarrhea
(2021修) Timentin (Ticarcillin/potassium clavulanate)：台灣目前無此藥
Dosage :
< 2000g and 0-7 days old : 150 mg/kg/day divided q12h
< 2000g and > 7 days old : 225 mg/kg/day divided q8h
2000g and 0-7 days old : 225 mg/kg/day divided q8h
2000g and > 7 days old : 300 mg/kg/day divided q6h
Term neonates and infants ＜3 months: 200-300mg ticarcillin/k/day div q6-8h
Infant ≧3 months and children:
Mild to moderate infection: 200mg ticarcillin/k/day div q6h
Severe infections (outside the CNS): 300mg ticarcillin/k/day div q4-6h; maximum dose:400mg/k/day not to exceed 18-24g/day
Dosing interval in renal impairment:
CCr 10-30: administer q8h
CCr <10: administer q12h
Dosing interval in hepatic impairment and CCr<10: administer q24h
Monitor Parameters: serum electrolytes, periodic renal, hepatic and hematologic function
Administration: IV intermittent infusion over 30min; final concentration for administration should not exceed 100mg/ml of ticarcillin; however, concentration ≦50mg/ml are preferred; if the patient is on concurrent aminoglycoside therapy, separate ticarcillin and clavulanate potassium administration from the aminoglycoside by at least 30-60min.
IV infusion：以sterile water for injection或D5W稀釋；IM：不可
Augmentin (Amoxicillin/Clavulanate)
Each 600mg vial contains Amoxicillin 500mg and Clavulanate 100mg
Dosage: preterm <7days, 25mg amoxicillin/kg/dose (30mg Augmentin [25Amo/5 clavulanate]) q12h; >7 days 25mg amoxicillin/kg/dose (30mg Augmentin) q8h
Solution compatibility: N/S
Administration: IV push 3-4mins or IV infusion 30mins
Concentrations: 1200mg/100ml NS=12 mg/ml

path: Infection / Infection / Antibiotics and Anti-Fungal agent 劑量 (Part 2)
content:
How to choose optimal drugs for fungus infection in NB? Balance the risk of infection vs drug toxicity. (請參考Infection-Neonatal candidiasis)
Clinical stable + previous antifungal (-) : try Fluconazole
Life threatening : Amphotericin-B use initially.

Dosage: Preterm neonates to infants< 3 months: 2mg/kg/dose once daily; IV infusion via syringe pump over at least 1 hour
Solution compatibility: N/S, 1/2 NS, 1/4 NS, LR(不可含glucose的溶液), max concentration 0.5mg/ml
Dosing adjustment in hepatic impairment： IV infusion via syringe pump over 1 hour
泡製後效期：24hrs(≦25℃) ; 48hrs(2-8℃)
Monitoring：K+, Ca2+, GOT/GPT
Adverse effects/ Precautions: thrombophlebitis, hypercalcemia, hypokalemia, elevated liver enzymes, and isolated direct hyperbilirubinemia.
若健保使用需先詢問主治醫師

Dosage: 3-5mg/kg/dose once daily; IV infusion
Solution compatibility: D10W(不可含有電解質的溶液)
泡製後濃度及效期：in D10W 0.2-2mg/ml 在2-8℃可放48小時
Monitoring: urine output, CBC for thrombocytopenia, electrolytes for hypokalemia, BUN/Cr and hepatic transaminases
Adverse effects/Precautions: anemia, thrombocytopenia, hypokalemia, nausea/vomiting, and fever/chills.
若健保使用需先詢問主治醫師

path: Infection / Infection / 周產期和新生兒期與新型冠狀病毒感染 (2022 增修)
content:
重點摘要 Reference: NeoReviews 2021;22 (5):e284-e295、台灣新生兒科醫學會指引第二版
孕婦感染COVID-19，比未懷孕婦女有更大機會進入重症加護病房並接受插管與呼吸支持、葉克膜與死亡率。
產前類固醇仍建議可用於感染的孕婦合併有早產風險 (包括34-36 6/7週) 時。
病毒主要透過呼吸道飛沫傳播，有三種可能機轉導致母體傳播SARS-CoV-2給嬰幼兒導致感染，子宮內傳播、生產過程中接觸到已感染母親之分泌物或糞便、產後接觸已感染母親或其他人員，子宮內傳播的機會較低，產後因接觸受感染母親之呼吸道分泌物是最可能的感染途徑。
接生時，新生兒科醫師需穿著全套個人防護設備。面罩通氣時以兩人操作為佳，其中一人以兩手固定面罩， 確保密合度、減少漏氣，另一人則持甦醒球或T-piece 甦醒 器通氣。在置入氣管內管時，可考慮使用影像喉頭鏡以減少風險。將染疫產婦之新生兒由產房轉送至新生兒加護病房或新生兒病房時，需預先決定路線，並將新生兒放置於封閉的保溫箱，將暴露最小化。備援人員應在DR/OR外部等待，在有需要時進入協助。
SARS-CoV-2陽性產婦的新生兒，出生一到三天內 SARS-CoV-2陽性者佔1.6%-3%；母乳哺餵與母嬰同室：目前沒有充足的證據顯示SARS-CoV-2可透過母乳傳染給新生兒。健康足月兒需在適當防護下進行母乳哺餵或母嬰同室，適當防護包括 (a) 在餵奶或親餵前，染疫母親應配戴外科口罩、以肥皂清洗雙手與乳房；也可由健康的照護者餵食擠出的母乳；(b) 在餵食以外的時間，嬰兒床/保溫箱需距離染疫母親的床至少6英尺(約180公分)，最好再隔一層物理屏障，如隔簾。請在與父母充分討論後，決定是否母乳哺餵與母嬰同室。
所有懷疑或確診 COVID-19 產婦產下之新生兒皆建議在出生後以二採為原則，建議出生後第24小時第一採，48-72小時第二採，兩次皆陰性者，新生兒若無症狀應盡早出院回家，但仍需居家隔離7天。
症狀開始已十天以上的染疫母親若燒退滿24小時(無使用退燒藥)，且症狀在改善，可安全無虞地照顧她的孩子。
COVID-19 感染新生兒相較於兒童重症率較高，主要是針對症狀給予支持性療法。抗病毒藥物與類固醇之使用無明確建議。新生兒較少出現多系統發炎症候群。
COVID-19 感染新生兒症狀
B. Stand-by 動線及注意事項
若產婦採非負壓隔離生產，則Stand-by時著一般裝備，不需特殊防疫裝備，或可視個人需求增加N95口罩。
若產婦採負壓隔離生產，則Stand-by時著乙級或丙級防疫裝備，請事先確認生產地點嬰兒處理台、氧氣供應及suction配置。用過的物品使用完請用漂白水消毒，靜置10分鐘即可。
媽媽為確診或疑似感染者，兒科醫師進入隔離室或進行呼吸治療需著乙級裝備，僅在前室或僅轉送者可著丙級裝備。
媽媽為COVID-19感染低風險，只是PCR報告未出時，兒科醫師著丙級裝備即可。
Stand-by備品盡量拿拋棄式ambu(含過濾器)及blade，掌上型monitor用夾鏈袋包覆。

Intraventricular hemorrhage (IVH)
Hydrocephalus
Neonatal Seizure
Hypoxic Ischemic Encephalopathy(HIE)
振幅整合腦電圖Amplitude EEG (aEEG)
NAS, neonatal abstinence syndrome
Neonatal stroke
Vein of Galen aneurysmal malformation

Definition
IVH is an intracranial hemorrhage that originates in the periventricular subependymal germinal matrix with subsequent entrance of blood into the ventricular system.
Timing
Early IVH: diagnosed at < 72h after birth
Late IVH: diagnosed after 72h of life
50% of IVH occurs in the 1st 6-12h of life, 75% by the 2nd day, 90% by the 3rd day
In 20% to 40%, the hemorrhage may extend during the first week of life.
Risk factors
Extreme prematurity /Presence of labor /Birth asphyxia /Need for vigorous resuscitation at birth/ Pneumothorax/Ventilated preterm infants, especially those who breathe out of synchrony with the ventilator/Seizures
Sudden elevation in arterial blood pressure as in rapid volume expansion and administration of hypertonic sodium bicarbonate
Hypothermia, hypotension, hypercarbia, acidosis, exchange transfusion, etc
Coagulation disturbances.
Incidence：Around 25% in infants weighing < 1500g; 60% in infants weighing <1000 g.
Grading：(Ref : Avery's diseases of the Newborn10th, p. 881)
Symptoms and signs
Asymptomatic (25-50%)
Subtle symptoms: eg. a bulging fontanelle, abrupt clinical  deterioration with hypotension, apnea, bradycardia, metabolic acidosis, anemia, seizures,  and change in muscle tone or level of consciousness
Diagnosis and follow-up
Brain sonography
Routine brain echo for VLBW:  DOL 3 & 7-10 d, or deterioration of clinical condition
If IVH is noted, repeat sonogram 1-2 weeks to assess the extent of hemorrhage
PVHI, periventricular hemorrhagic infarction: IVH後會造成腦室周圍白質 venous infarction
Gr. IV IVH 會在 1-8 wks內因為 tissue destruction 形成 porencephalic cyst
Brain CT and MRI are acceptable alternatives (more expansive and require transport)
Treatment
Supportive care : volume expanders and pharmacologic agents to support BP, 
assisted ventilation to correct hypoxemia and hypercarbia, and RBC transfusion to correct anemia
Anticonvulsant therapy for seizure.
Repeated spinal tap or shunt operation for posthemorrhagic hydrocephalus (PHH)
Prevention of IVH with early indomethacin (缺藥, 不常規使用) (Ref: Pediatrics Apr 1994)
0.1mg/kg qd x3days (1st  dose at 6-12 postnatal hours,  then every 24 hours for 2nd 3rd doses)
In D5W 2cc iv drip > 30 mins扣IV if U.O. >0.5ml/kg/hr and no contraindication
Prognosis: (Ref : Avery's diseases of the Newborn10th, p. 933, 883)
30-50% of neonates with Gr. 3-4 IVH develop PHVD (posthemorrhagic ventricular dilatation) and 20-40% of them will need VP shunt
Neurologic sequelae occur in 35% of Gr. 3 IVH and 55% Gr. 4 IVH

Classification
Noncommunicating hydrocephalus: aqueductal stenosis, Dandy-Walker cyst, tumors, ventricular inflammation and scars
Communicating hydrocephalus : Arnold-Chiari malformation, encephalocele, leptomeningeal inflammations (infection, hemorrhage), lissencephaly, congenitalabsence of arachnoid granulations, tumors, overproduction of CSF (choroidplexus papilloma)
Signs
If increased intracranial pressure is present, the infant may show signs of apnea, bradycardia, irritability, lethargy, vomiting, tense fontanelle, widely split sutures, dilated scalp veins, rapid head growth, and setting-sun sign
Papilledema is rarely observed in neonates
Diagnosis
Intrauterine ultrasonographic screening
Neonatal ultrasonographic screening for premature infants
VLBW / Larger, sick premature infants
Rapidly enlarging head circumference / Signs of increased intracranial pressure
CT scan or MRI of the head
Management
Congenital aqueductal stenosis or neural tube defects
Decompression by prompt placement of a ventricular bypass shunt into anintracranial or extracranial compartment
Ventriculoperitoneal shunts are considered the method of choice
Posthemorrhagic hydrocephalus, PHH
Mild hydrocephalus usually arrests within 4 weeks of progressive ventricular dilatation or returns to normal size within the first few months of life
Severe, rapidly progressive hydrocephalus :, external ventricular drain (EVD), ventriculosubgaleal shunt (VSGS), ventricular access device(VAD)  and VP shunt
Posthemorrhagic hydrocephalus of prematurity management protocol

Differentiation between jitteriness and seizure
Jitteriness is often precipitated by stimuli such as moving the infant, suctioning, or noise
If the abnormal movement can be stopped by manipulating the infant, particularly by flexing the involved limbs, it is probably jitteriness
The jitteriness is of equal amplitude in both moving direction, however, the seizure consists of a rapid movement in one direction, with a slower recovery phase in the other
Jitteriness is not accompanied by abnormal eye movements or other subtle signs
Classification：Subtle, clonic (focal, multifocal), tonic (focal, generalized), myoclonic (focal, multifocal, generalized)
Etiology
Perinatal asphyxia, hypoxic-ischemic encephalopathy
Intracranial hemorrhage / trauma
Subarachnoid hemorrhage / periventricular or intraventricular hemorrhage
Subdural hemorrhage
Central nervous system infections / congenital malformations
Cerebrovascular syndromes
Metabolic diseases (hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, electrolyte disturbances: hypo- and hypernatremia)
Drug-related disorders (ex: heroin, methadone, alcohol)
Benign neonatal seizures
Manifestations
Ocular: staring, pupillary dilation or constriction, tonic deviation of eyes, nystagmus, blinking, eyelid fluttering
Oral: sucking, smacking, drooling, chewing, tongue twitching, tongue protrusion, crying, grunting
Changes in vital signs: bradycardia, tachycardia, hypotension, hypertension, arrhythmia, apnea, shallow respiration, hyperpnea, irregular respiration
Color: flushing, cyanosis, pallor, mottling
Abnormal movements and tone: multifocal twitching, focal twitching, generalized myoclonus, focal myoclonus, swimming movements, bicycling movements, hypotonia, hypertonia, and opisthotonus, etc.

Dextrostix, glucose, Ca2+, Mg2+, Na+, K+, BUN/Cr, GOT/GPT, ABG, NH3, PT/PTT, CBC/DC, amino acid in urine and plasma, etc.
Ultrasonography, CT or MRI of the head
Blood culture if infection is suspected
CSF for culture, routine , glucose and protein if meningitis is suspected
EEG
Management
Keep airway patent, adequate ventilation and circulation
Glucose IV
Anticonvulsants

ILAE + 小兒神經科最新建議, 請掃右方 QR code
Phenobarbital (Luminal)  First choice!
Loading dose : 建議從 loading 10 mg/kg/dose, IM or IV(ELBW or bleeding tendency). 
If necessary-additional dose : 5 mg/kg/dose IM/IV to a maximum of 20 mg/kg
Maintenance dose : GA  ≧ 30wks 3-8 mg/kg/day
GA＜30wks 1-3mg/kg/day IM or  IV Q12H
Adverse effects : sedation, lethargy, paradoxical excitement, GI upset, ataxia, rash, Steven-Johnson syndrome
Luminal drug level monitoring：(Ref: Micromedex)
Newborn Luminal T1/2 = 60h-180h
Therapeutic level : 15-40 mcg/ml (Ref : Avery’s Diseases of the Newborn 9th edition)
驗藥物濃度的時間：
For suspect intoxication→ 隨時都可以驗藥化濃度!
For susepct 劑量不足→ 至少3-4個半衰期後再驗；急的話，至少５天後再驗
藥物濃度報告出來 → 與主治醫師、小兒神經科、NI/NBC藥師討論→調劑量
Levetiracetem (Keppra) (500mg/5ml vial, 100mg/ml oral solution)
Loading dose : 20 mg/kg/dose, IVD 30min, 可以 loading 2 times
Maintenance dose : 30-60 mg/kg/day q12h
IV diluted in D5W or NS, max concentration: 15mg/ml, drip 15-30 mins
Adverse effects : somnolence, irritability
Phenytoin (Dilantin) (50mg/ml)
Dosage: 建議從 loading 10mg/kg/dose, maintenance 2.5mg/kg/dose q12h開始
Loading dose: 20 mg/kg/dose IVD (1 mg/kg/min) NI常用IV slowly push
Maintenance dose: 3~8 mg/kg /day IV q12h
Diluted in an electrolyte solution (in dextrose water→precipitation)
Therapeutic level: 10-20 mcg/mL
Adverse effects : extravasation causes inflammation and necrosis, other rare in neonates, nystagmus, ataxia, lethargy, slurred speech, diplopia, headache, hirsutism, behavior changes, gum hyperplasia
Midazolam (Dormicum) :
Dosage：(5mg/ml amp)
Give 0.15 mg/kg iv bolus and l μg/kg/min IV continuous infusion, with increment of 1 μg/kg/min every 15 min until seizures stop or up to the upper limit of 10μg/kg/min.
Adverse effects : miosis, respiratory depression, drowsiness, bradycardia, hypotension, constipation, sedation, GI upset, urine retention, histamine release, sweating, physiologic dependent
泡法：6×BW mg in D10W 10 ml，1ml/hr=10 ug/kg/min，Max:10ug/kg/min
Lorazepam (Ativan) (Reference: NEOFAX, 2011)
Usual dosage : 0.05-0.1 mg/kg/dose IV slowly push
Repeat doses based on clinical response
Adverse effects : Respiratory depression, rhythmic myoclonic jerking has occurred in premature neonates receiving lorazepam for sedation
Valproic acid (Depakine) : 小於兩歲不建議使用，因肝毒性明顯

Etiology
Antepartum : maternal hypotension, infertility treatment, thyroid disease
Intrapartum : maternal fever, difficult forceps delivery, breech extraction, cord prolapse, abruptio placentae etc.
Postnatal : severe respiratory distress, congenital heart disease, sepsis, and shock etc.
Clinical presentations
Symptoms usually evolve over a period of 72 hours
First 12 hours : not easily arousable, periodic breathing with apnea or bradycardia, intact pupillary responses, hypotonia, Moro/grasp/suck/swallow reflex may be absent or depressed, seizure (+/-)
12-24 hours :  increase in the level of alertness, seizure, apnea, jitteriness, weakness in the proximal limbs, exaggerated Moro reflex and DTR
24-72 hours : level of consciousness deteriorates, followed by respiratory arrest and signs of brainstem dysfunction such as loss of responsiveness to the doll’s eyes maneuver, fixed and dilated pupils
Diagnosis (Ref : Avery’s Diseases of the Newborn 9th edition)  2021 revised
Metabolic abnormalities, cerebral dysgenesis, and infection can mimic HIE
History of intrauterine distress, eg. abnormalities on the fetal heart tracing, passage of meconium, or a history of difficult labor or delivery with decrease in placental or fetal blood flow; history of a difficult resuscitation
Laboratory studies may show metabolic complications, such as hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, hyponatremia, and acidosis
EEG, brain echo, brain MRI (Ref: Arch Pediatr Adolesc Med. 2012;166(7):634-640)
MRI 建議事件發生後第5-7或第10-14天做，因為第7-10天有pseudonormalization，但實際檢查時間須配合臨床情形，如呼吸器使用與否來決定
Brain CT often is not useful
Treatment
Supportive care
Oxygen to prevent further hypoxia during a seizure or secondary to prolonged apnea, in severe cases, mechanical ventilation may be necessary
Plasma expanders for hypotension
Correct acidosis
Maintain blood sugar at least 45 mg/dl
Fluid to maintain urine output, D10W at 65 ml/kg/day is recommended at day 1 (but fluid requirement will depend on the presence of asphyxia-induced renal  failure)
Medications :
Anticonvulsants for seizure: phenobarbital, phenytoin, midazolam
Erythropoietin (EPO) alfa (4000 IU/amp, NT811/amp) 自費使用
Dosage for HIE: 1000IU/kg/day at DOL 1, 2, 3, 5, 7 (Ref: NEATO trial, Wu el al., 2012&2016)
Dosage for PVL: 2500 IU/kg/day qd x 3 days
Slowly iv push (over 1-5 mins) or SC, 不可IV drip or IM
Therapeutic hypothermia (NRP 6th Ed., Nelson 19th Ed.) 快速鍵"cooling"

path: Neurology / Neurology / 健保給付規定：
content:
須符合以下3項：1)出生週數≧36週, 2)事件發生後6小時內實施, 3)出生後有「中等嚴重度(stage 2)」至「重度(stage 3)」腦病變之證據
「且」有下列「任一」項之情形：1)出生後一小時內嚴重酸血症，血液PH值≦7或base deficit ≧16mmol/L(動脈血或靜脈血均可)。 2) 出生10分鐘時的APGAR分數≦5分。3) 出生後持續急救至少10分鐘。
註：不完全符合健保給付標準者，如出生週數介於34-36週間、出生體重>1800gm克等，是否仍實施自費低溫治療，可依個別案例與主治醫師討論後決定。

path: Neurology / Neurology / 振幅整合腦電圖Amplitude EEG (aEEG)
content:
(Reference: Shah NA, Wusthoff CJ. Arch Dis Child Educ Pract Ed 2015;100: 75–81)
使用目的：提供即時、持續、簡便監測腦部背景活動的工具
適用對象：疑腦傷或腦病變需監測大腦功能者
醫囑開立：
Combine or consult pediatric neurologist (Call CR)
開awake & sleep EEG單，聯絡腦波室安排完整的EEG檢查才會有正式報告
aEEG 使用
去小兒神經生理檢查室借aEEG 機器並記得填寫借閱本
(2025增) 值班或假日時間須急借用的話，請聯絡Neuro CR借鑰匙
導線黏貼及軟體操作可詢問NB or Neuro CR
請事先將頭皮清潔乾淨(建議使用磨砂膏)，若導極與頭皮黏貼不夠密合，易有干擾波影響判讀
簡易的aEEG需貼C3,P3, C4,P4及reference 和ground電極，監測左右各一通道
基本判讀
畫面分上下兩半(如下圖)，上半部是左右大腦即時的原始腦波(raw EEG)，下半部是以15秒為單位形成的振幅整合腦電圖 (aEEG)，把每15秒內最高及最低的振幅以一直線表現
aEEG可分三大面向判讀：背景形態(Background pattern)、痙攣訊號
(Seizure activity)、以及睡眠喚醒週期(Sleep-wake cycle)
背景形態：
根據振幅的活動頻帶可分為下列五種形態：Continuous, discontinuous, burst suppression, low voltage, flat trace
Z
背景腦部功能異常嚴重度可依照振幅或是形態去定義
正常：aEEG 的活動頻帶的下側邊緣在>5μV。睡眠喚醒週期存在。
輕微異常：aEEG的活動頻帶的下側邊緣在>5μV。睡眠喚醒週期不存在。
中度異常：aEEG的活動頻帶上側邊緣在>10μV，下側邊緣在<5μV。
嚴重異常： aEEG 的活動頻帶上側邊緣在<10μV，下側邊緣在<5μV。
中度的腦病變：非連續性的形態Discontinuous pattern
重度的腦病變：猝發壓抑  Burst suppression，連續性低電壓Continuous low voltage，平坦波Flat trace
痙攣訊號：與aEEG有關的痙攣發作特點是電壓突然增加，伴隨著縮小的aEEG活動頻帶，並隨後會有一段短暫的發作後抑制期間，在原始腦波raw EEG可見異常。可分為Single, Repetitive, Status epilepticus。
注意：若aEEG看到疑似seizure訊號，應調閱當時的raw EEG，若無異常波型就不是seizure。如下左圖。
睡眠喚醒週期：睡眠時期的振幅較寬，清醒時則較窄。可分類為Developed, Immature, Absent。如上右圖。
aEEG 的臨床應用
aEEG 形態符合 mod HIE 定義者，於 severe HIE 相比，對低溫療法的反應較好。
在腦傷後的3-6小時的aEEG可預測18個月後的無嚴重殘疾的存活率：
若aEEG正常，18個月時的無嚴重殘疾的存活率為100%，
若aEEG有重度異常，18個月時的中重度殘疾或死亡率為59%。
若接受低溫療法的病嬰在出生48小時後aEEG轉為正常（EEG形態，以及正常的睡眠清醒週期）、預後較好。
以aEEG來偵測發作正確率只有40-76%，只能用於篩檢，也可能會有偽陰性的可能。不能用於確診發作。
偵測Subclinical seizure。接受低溫療法的病嬰約有34%會有seizure，而其中43%屬於subclinical seizure是臨床上看不到症狀的。(Ref: Nash et al. 2011)

path: Neurology / Neurology / Treatment for Neonatal abstinence syndrome(NAS) (2024年修) (Part 1)
content:
Check NAS score q 4 h, 但若NAS score > 8, 則q 2 h check
請掃下列 QR code 參考 NAS 評分項目
Medication
Neonatal opium solution (NOS): 將opium tincture 以Distilled water稀釋25倍(=0.4mg/ml of morphine) (MMH目前沒有)
Morphine Sulfate (15mg): 0.5# in D/W 7.5ml=1mg/ml of morphine 冰箱保存一週
Side effect: sleepiness, constipation, poor suck, and ultimately profound narcosis with obtundation, hypothermia, respiratory depression, apnea and bradycardia
If this occurs, stop the medication until the abstinence scores >8.
Add Phenobarbital to control irritability when the NOS is > 2 ml/kg/day
Phenobarbital (5-8 mg/kg/day)
IM or PO TID, and taper by 10% each day after improvement of symptoms.
Drug of choice if the infant is thought to be withdrawing from a nonnarcotic drug or from multiple drug use.
Does not control the diarrhea that occurs with withdrawal.
Using Phenobarbital with NOS allows a lower dose of NOS and lessens the side effects.
Weaning:
If NAS score <8 for 1-2 days→wean by 10% of total dose q 1-2 days
If NAS score >8 after weaning→restart the last effective dose
DC when the daily dose of NOS < 0.3 ml/kg/day or Morphine Sulfate<0.12ml/kg/day
Observe the infant for 2-3 days under no medication control prior to discharge.
If the scores are too low, make sure that the infant is not overdosed.
Neonatal stroke 2025新增
Classification (clinical-anatomic subtypes)
Arterial ischemic stroke
Hemorrhagic stroke
Cerebral sinovenous thrombosis (CSVT)
Periventricular venous infarction (PVI)
Clinical presentations: Encephalopathy, seizure, abnormal tone, focal neurologic deficits, respiratory impairments(apnea), and feeding problems
Diagnosis
Define lesion
Cranial doppler sonogram, brain CT
MRI (greater sensitivity and specificity)
DWI may underestimate full extent of infarction: first 24 h and after day 5
T2 & T2 FLAIR more reliable than DWI at day 7 and later in term newborns
Neurovascular imaging: MRA, MRV
Investigate underlying cause
CBC/DC, PLT, CRP, B/C, ABG, blood group, BUN/Cr, GOT/GPT, CK/CKMB, dextrose, PT/APTT, D-dimmer, Fibrinogen, FDP
Collage /EPI closure time
Factor XIII Activity (自費)
Von Willebrand factor (vWF Activity)
Anti-Thrombin III (AT-III), Protein-C/S test
Echocardiogram: detect structural heart disease with right- to-left shunt
Management
Supportive care
Adequate oxygenation and ventilation
Correct dehydration and anemia
Monitor and correct metabolic disturbances(acidosis, hypoglycemia, hypocalcemia, and electrolyte disorders)
Antiseizure medications
Prevent further injury
In acute CSVT: consider anticoagulation with unfractionated heparin or LMWH
For intracerebral hemorrhage:
Correction of markedly low platelet counts
Replacement of deficient coagulation factors for neonates with coagulation factor deficiency
Ventricular drainage or shunting for hydrocephalus
Vein of Galen aneurysmal malformation (VGAM) 2025新增
Classification
Primary

path: Neurology / Neurology / Treatment for Neonatal abstinence syndrome(NAS) (2024年修) (Part 2)
content:
choroidal type:Multiple feeders, including: thalamoperforating, choroidal and pericallosal arteries are located in the subarachnoid space in the choroidal fissure
mural type:Fistulae in the subarachnoid space in the wall of the median prosencephalic vein
Secondary:Parenchymal Arteriovenous Malformations
Clinical presentations: The greater the size, the larger the degree of shunting through the lesion, and the earlier the presentation.
CV : high-output cardiac failure, pulmonary hypertension, and  multiorgan system failure
Neuro: Cyanosis, hemorrhagic lesions, hydrocephalus, cerebral atrophy,impaired cortical development
Diagnosis
Prenatal screening ultrasound or with the fetal MRI
Cranial doppler sonogram
Brain CT with
MRI
Investigate for severity of  heart failure
Echocardiogram
Renal and liver function test
EEG
Management: The main therapy goal is to minimize congestive heart failure
Bicêtre score
< 8 points：No treatment (recent studies showed this group receiving embolization may still have good outcome)
8-12 points：Emergent embolization
>12 points：Embolization at 5 months of age with continue management
Embolization：the main approach to treatment and can be performed by an arterial transarterial approach using liquid adhesive agents or micro coils or by a transvenous approach typically using the umbilical or femoral veins.
Microsurgery： not a primary treatment, but may still be performed  for  persistent hydrocephalus, intracranial bleeds, hematomas, or when embolization fails

Acute Renal Failure
Hematuria
Urinary Tract Infection
Newborn CCr formula, Creatine ref
Peritoneal Dialysis
新生兒 CVVH Protocol
Nephrolithiasis

Anuria or oliguria with an associated increase in serum creatinine.
It is recognized now that acute renal failure in the neonate can present with anuria (<0.5 ml/kg/hr), oliguria (<1 ml/kg/hr), normal urine output (1-5 ml/kg/hr) or polyuria (>5 ml/kg/hr)

Prerenal Causes
Decreased plasma volume: hemorrhage, dehydration, sepsis
Other causes of renal hypoperfusion: hypoxia, RDS, CHF, shock
Intrinsic (renal) Causes
Congenital renal anomalies: Bil.agenesis, bilateral multicystic (dysplastic) kidneys (MCDK), infantile polycystic kidney disease (ARPKD, ADPKD)
Vascular lesions: renal artery thrombosis, renal vein thrombosis
Ischemic: shock, hemorrhage, dehydration, sepsis, hypoxia, RDS
Nephrotoxic: aminoglycoside antibiotics
Uric Acid: hyperuricemia in the newborn
Postrenal Causes
Bilateral obstruction: posterior urethral valves, trauma to urethra, imperforate prepuce, urethral diverticulum, neurogenic bladder, megacystis, megaureter syndrome
Obstruction of only functioning kidney ureteropelvic junction obstruction
Incidence: 1%-8% (some studies, up to 23%), prerenal factor as the cause in 73%

Decreased or absent urine output
Family history: urinary tract disease (congenital anomalies), oligohydramnios
Physical examination
Abdominal mass: distended bladder, hydronephrosis, polycystic kidney

Meningomyelocele
Pulmonary hypoplasia
Urinary ascites
Prune belly: 先天性腹肌缺失

Bladder catheterization: to check the existence of urine in the bladder
Lab. study:
serum BUN and creatinine
Urinary index:
CBC/DC + platelet: thrombocytopenia
Electrolyte: K↑
Urinalysis: hematuria (renal vein thrombosis, tumors, DIC), pyuria (UTI)
Reference: Avery’s diseases of the newborn 10th ed p 1287
Diagnostic fluid challenge (see treatment)
Radiologic studies
Abdominal ultrasonography
Intravenous urography: limited usefulness
Abdominal X-ray study
Radionuclide scanning: DMSA, DTPA
Reference: Avery’s diseases of the newborn 10th ed p 1281

Fluid
The “Fluid challenge” is perhaps the most useful diagnostic test (重建kidney perfusion 很重要)
“Appropriate” volume expander: 10-20 ml/kg。ex: N/S, blood products, 5% albumin
Contraindication of fluid challenge and mannitol: fluid overload, CHF, or severe hypertension
Dopamine, low dose (2-5 mcg/kg/min) (less therapeutic value currently)
If no response is obtained (>2 mL/kg of urine per hour)-> diuretic
if furosemide ineffective, should be discontinued to avoid the associated risk of ototoxicity

Single dose: 1 mg/kg/dose, (range: 0.5~2 mg/kg/dose Q6-12H)
Continuous: 0.1 - 0.2 mg/kg/hr, 以12~24hrs U/O調整,每次0.1 mg/kg/hr, Max 0.4~0.5 mg/kg/hr
Burinex：單次最大劑量或許可以高至0.1 mg/kg/dose
Preterm：0.01- 0.06 mg/kg/dose Q12-48H ; Term：0.01-0.05 mg/kg/dose Q12-48H
Prostaglandin E1 may at as a diuretic in neonates and is useful in increasing renal perfusion and naturesis.  Prostaglandin is not used extensively for this purpose.
If no  response is obtained -> Obstruction should be ruled out
Place a lubricated 5-8 Fr feeding tube in the urethra
Renal eho

weighting the patient twice a day
計算 I/O, total output =  insensible water loss + urine and GI losses
Insensible water loss ( a normal newborn ) = 50 ml/kg/day
The goal of fluid restriction may be impossible → Peritoneal dialysis or CAVH (Continuous arteriovenous hemofiltration) or CAVHD (Continuous arteriovenous hemofiltration and dialysis)
Correct electrolyte imbalance and metabolic acidosis
Follow serum Na and K levels
IV bicarbonate therapy if the pH < 7.25 or serum HCO3- < 12 mEq
Restrict protein < 2g/kg/day and ensure adequate nonprotein caloric intake
Follow BP serially: always at risk for developing chronic H/T
Although BP initially may be low in neonatal acute renal failure, mild hypertension is common later.
Secondary to the specific diagnoses of polycystic kidney disease and renal artery thrombosis, the H/T may be more acute and more severe.
For practical purposes, significant H/T in the term infant: BP >100/70; Premature infant: >90/60
UAC lines are perhaps the most frequent cause of renal arterial occlusion in the neonate. The infusion solutions usually contain heparin at 0.2-1 unit/mL
Initial management consists of fluid and electrolyte control.
Antihypertensive agents:
Diuretic therapy is inappropriate in patients with ARF.
ACE inhibitors, used with caution, are a good first approach; they have excellent efficacy in the H/T associated with renal artery thrombosis.
Life threatening H/T in the neonate can be treated with diazoxide or nitroprusside.
Dialysis

Presence of gross or microscopic blood in the urine
A red-stained diaper: may due to bile pigmented, porphyrins or urates
Incidence not a common problem in newborns

Diagnosis
Lab. Study : urinalysis/urine culture/ serum BUN, Cr/ coagulation study : PT, PTT, Thrombin time
Ultrasonography/IVP/nuclear scans
Management：directed at the underlying cause

0.5-1.0 % in term infants ; 3-5 % in preterm infants or BW < 2500g
Note: male>female incidence in neonatal period

UTI presents in an infant 1 y/o with associated urinary tract abnormality found in about 50 % of neonates
The most common organisms are gram-negative rods, E. coli.
In neonates, UTI are frequently acquired by hematogenous spread
Risk Factors 
indwelling urinary catheters, systemic sepsis, urinary tract obstruction, neurogenic bladder, male newborns

Signs of sepsis
Nonspecific findings：poor feeding, vomiting, jaundice, failure to thrive

urinalysis
urine culture: bladder aspiration/catheterization
blood culture, CBC/DC, serum bilirubin

Initial antibiotics treatment：IV Ampicillin + gentamicin until definitive U/C and B/C
Further investigations: renal /bladder ultrasound, VCUG, renal scan/IVP (for complex problems)

One-fourth of infants can have recurrent UTI within the first year of life
Effective use of long-term suppressive antibiotics (in VUR ) + indicated corrective surgery reduce incidence of renal scarring and renal insufficiency

Newborn CCr算法: GFR = K * height (cm)/ Cr (plasma)
Lab data (Ref: 1. Nelson 17 th edition, 2. Pediatric dosage handbook 16 th edition 3. Manual of neonatal care 6 th edition)
Creatinine level (mg/dL) Ref: Journal of Perinatology (2008) 28, 226–229

適應症(Indications)
急性腎衰竭(Acute renal failure)
嚴重或有症狀的電解質不平衡(Electrolyte imbalance)：高血鉀症(Hyperkalemia)
酸血症(Severe acidosis)
水分儲積太多(Fluid overload)：高血壓、鬱血性心衰竭或肺水腫
嚴重的腎功能不全(Renal insufficiency)：BUN>100 mg/dL或Creatinine上升太快
有尿毒的症狀：如腦病變(Uremic encephalopathy)
毒物的移除（如先天代謝異常導致的高氨血症等）
禁忌症(Contraindications) ：臍膨出(omphalocele)、腹裂(gastroschisis)、橫膈疝氣、膀胱外翻、腹膜沾黏、NEC、VP shunt、剛動過腹膜手術仍有引流管時
外科beside放PD管時參考用藥
Fentanyl 0.04 cc/kg/dose
Atracurium 0.3 mg/kg/dose
Ketamine 0.02 cc/kg/dose
操作步驟
於superior anterior iliac crest與肚臍連線外1/3處定位。
消毒，鋪洞巾。以22號IC自肚臍與symphysis pubis連線上1/3處插入，拔出硬管，將軟管推入，注入warm normal saline (10ml/Kg)。
以Xylocaine局部麻醉，於定位點劃0.5 cm 的平行傷口，先縫一針以利後續固定，並將下方的脂肪組織盡量撐開。以外包PD tube的 trocar 垂直插入，直到出現裂痕聲，表示已穿破腹膜進入腹腔。將trocar稍往外拉，以免傷及內臟，再將trocar與PD tube一起朝Douglas pouch向下插入至底，此時先前打入的 normal saline 應由PD tube流出。將多餘的tube 剪掉，留下約1cm的PD tube以利固定位置，接上Y型管並縫合傷口
照一張Plain abdomen film 看 PD tube位置是否正確。
每隔三天應重換PD導管，以防感染。
若PD tube流出不順，可打入normal saline沖洗，絕不可回抽，否則易吸住mesentery，造成 bleeding。若流出的PD solution 呈鮮紅色，則每CC加1～2單位的Heparin 沖洗，直到流出液變clear為止
PD solution可分為1.5％/2.5％/4.25％ glucose三種濃度,濃度越高者,脫水效果越好。
PD order: 通常以10cc/kg/cycle 開始, 然後漸加至15cc/kg/cycle, 最高不要超過20cc/kg/cycle
PD solution 放置於腹腔時間愈久,脫水效果愈差。如欲脫水者,可縮短透析時間(如每個小時換液一次)。
Drain 時間：若位置正確，功能良好時，10～15分鐘即可。
若要Hold Peritoneal dialysis時, 要先將PD管路密閉不可打開。

path: Nephrology / Nephrology / 新生兒 CVVH Protocol
content:
適應症
急性腎臟衰竭：無尿期超過48小時、高血鉀且伴有心電圖之變化、嚴重高尿酸血症、尿毒性腦病變
敗血症：移除發炎物質
猛爆性肝炎: bridge to liver transplantation
身體體液過多、充血性心臟衰竭
代謝急症：楓糖尿症，脂肪酸代謝異常，尿素循環代謝異常，丙酸血症
誤食毒性物質
處理流程
與兒童腎臟科醫師共照
聯絡心臟血管外科置放血液透析導管/腹膜透析管
聯絡 CVVH 廠商 (機器及管路安裝)
醫囑開立：快速鍵-CVVH/CVVH A 液加KCl: CVVHKCL
常見問題處理及參數調整
血壓低:
降低 blood flow rate (過低容易形成血栓)
降低脫水量
跨膜壓增加
降低 ultrafiltration rate
更換管路並考慮加上或是增加heparin劑量
附錄 (一)、血液透析導管
管路選擇
MedComp 8Fr Double Lumen - NICU 常備一組，其他常備單位: PICU
Arrow 16Ga One Way CVC - NICU 常備兩組，其他常備單位: 麻醉科
Arrow 4Fr Two Way CVC - NICU 常備
MedComp 7Fr CVC **罕病基金會 **
施打部位
Double Lumen: 右內頸/右外頸/左內外頸/左右股靜脈
Single Lumen: trans-diaphragmatic
管路維護
透析靜脈導管
預防導管內血栓形成-On heparin lock ： 以1ml 5000U 之 Heparin不稀釋，推入管路上標示priming量，on lock
預防 Staphyloccus/ Streptococcus 感染：Heparin- Vancomycin lock （25mcg/mL Vancomycin & Heparin 100U/L）
中央導管
以普通點滴持續輸注使用
若管路已阻塞：Urokinase 1amp (60,000) dilute with N/S to 12ml, prime the lumen with 0.6ml, and on lock immediately. Hold the solution in the lumen for 15 minutes. Repeat up to 3 times.
Heparin pump 泡法: BW (kg) * 1ml (5000U) Heparin in 500ml N/S, run 1ml/hr (10U/kg/hr)

Incidence: 7~41% in prematurity
Pathogenesis:
Risk factor:
Prematurity (especially male, GA<32wks, VLBW)
目前本院常規針對 ELBW (BW < 1000gm) infants 在出院前會安排 Kidney echo
Renal tubule immaturity
Increased likelihood of hypercalciuria, hypocitraturia
Nephrogenesis：Calcium oxalate crystal adherence to hyaluronan, osteopontin, and CD44 expressing injured/regenerating tubular epithelial cells
Lower GFR
Lower fluid flow rate within the tubular lumen
VLBW : decreased bicarbonate reabsorptive capacity
Drug exposure: Furosemide (especially > 410 mg/kg/day , the strongest risk factor of nephrocalcinosis), Dexamethasone, xanthine(theophylline, caffeine), aminoglycoside
Length of assisted ventilation and Oxygen therapy
Length of hospital stay
Formula and parenteral feeding
Genitourinary anomalies
30% of children with urolithiasis
50% with metabolic abnormality：Hypercalciuria, 34~50%、Hyperoxaluria, 10~20%、Hypocitraturia, 10%、Cystinuria, 5~20%、Hyperuricosuria, 2~20%
Symptoms and signs
acute, severe flank pain, radiation to groin are uncommon, particularly in children < 5 years
Macroscopic or microscopic hematuria, nonspecific pain localized to the abdomen, flank, or pelvis
may be confused with colic pain
Renal stones may be found incidentally
UTI
Lab evaluation
Urine
Blood：Ca, Mg, P, Cre, uric acid；If hypercalcemia→parathyroid hormone, 25(OH)-D
Treatment
Increase fluid intake,  at least 1.5 to 2 L/m2/d
Keep U/O> 750ml/day in infant
Decrease sodium intake
Lower Ca/P intake；Dietary calcium restriction is not recommended since it can increase intestinal oxalate absorption
No restriction of protein
Change loop diuretics to Thiazide
Magnesium supplement

path: Nephrology / Nephrology / Correction of low Vit D
content:
額外藥物見下表 (Dr. 蔡: potassium citrate 0.5~1 meq/kg/day)
Ref: Urolithiasis in Children, Pediatr Clin N Am 59 (2012) 881–896
Current Trends, Evaluation, and Management of Pediatric Nephrolithiasis, JAMA Pediatr. 2015;169(10):964-970

Hyperbilirubinemia of neonates
Unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia
Exchange Transfusion
Conjugated hyperbilirubinemia + cholestasis 藥物
Prolonged jaundice
Contrast enema
Stoma care 
Trust Guideline for the Management of Stoma Output in Neonates and Infant
IHPS management algorithm
GI drugs

Bilirubin
Sources: heme-containing protein
Major 75% from hemoglobin, 
1 gm Hb → 34 mg bilirubin
Other 25%- “early-label hemoglobin”
from ineffective erythropoiesis in the bone marrow
from other heme containing protein in tissues, eg: myoglobin, peroxidase...
from free heme
Normal bilirubin production rate in normal newborn 6-10 mg/kg/day, higher than in adult (3-4 mg/kg/day) because
Greater RBC mass
Shorter RBC life span by nearly 25%
Metabolism
Transport in blood
Unconjugated bilirubin (bilirubin IX alpha) is a weak acid and lipophilic
Unconjugated bilirubin is bound to albumin in blood (1g albumin binds 8g bilirubin in term infants, less in preterm infants)
Albumin binding sites may be occupied by other agents (eg. valium) and the binding may be reduced by decreased pH
Metabolism by hepatocytes
Unconjugated bilirubin is taken up by hepatocytes, bound by carrier proteins (including Ligandin) and conjugated by uridine diphosphate glucuronyl transferase (UDPG–T)
Conjugated bilirubin is water soluble
Phenobarbital can increase the concentration of hepatic Ligandin and UDPG–T
Excretion and re-uptake
Conjugated bilirubin is excreted into the biliary tract and enters the intestinal lumen
Although bacteria may convert conjugated bilirubin into water soluble stercobilin, this does not appreciably occur in the sterile gut of the neonate
Beta–glucuronidase in the newborn gut degrades conjugated bilirubin producing unconjugated form allowing reabsorption and binding to serum albumin (enterohepatic recirculation)
Persistent enterohepatic recirculation of bilirubin can significantly contribute to hyperbilirubinemia in infants without stool output
Physiologic vs non–physiologic (pathologic) hyperbilirubinemia
Physiologic (non–pathologic or “normal jaundice”)
Unconjugated bilirubin levels (Ref: Nelson21th Table 123.4)
Full term infants:  peak <12 mg/dl
Preterm infants:  peak < 15 mg/dl
Etiology
Increased unconjugated bilirubin formation
Difficulty of hepatocytes removing unconjugated bilirubin
Continued enterohepatic cycling despite lack of maternal clearance
Pathologic (黃疸處置建議手冊1st p. 22)
早發性：在第一天大就出現的黃疸
上升太快：膽紅素上升速率一天超過 5 mg/dL，或是一小時超過 0.2 mg/dL
數值過高：總膽紅素值超過同年齡群 (以出生後小時或天數計) 正常分佈的
第 95 百分位以上
膽汁滯留：直接型膽紅素超過 1.5-2 mg/dL (有文獻建議 1mg/dl)，或是超過
總膽紅素的 20% (15%-Avery’s diseases of Newborn 10th ed, p.1099)
延遲性黃疸：足月兒超過二星期或是早產兒超過三星期仍有黃疸
Unconjugated hyperbilirubinemia (indirect hyperbilirubinemia)
Introduction: the most common form in the newborn
Elevations of indirect serum bilirubin are related to bilirubin load more than bilirubin excretion and are therefore dependent on the GA and chronological age of the infant.
Complication:
Fat-soluble bilirubin stains basal ganglion → cerebral palsy
Staining of the basal ganglion is kernicterus
Lab studies:
Blood smear, CBC, ABO & HDNB (= direct and indirect Coomb’s test), 
reticulocyte count, G-6-PD
Sepsis work-up: only when the patient show other signs of sepsis

Anti-A and anti-B antibodies (IgG): for suspected ABO incompatibility
Rh antigens (mother’s and newborn’s) for positive Coombs’ test
Treatment
Stop agents that displace bilirubin from albumin (ex: NSAIDs, ceftriaxone)
Ensure metabolic stability
Feeding if possible
Evaluate and treat sepsis if indicated
Phototherapy 可參考routine章節
Rationale
Reduce serum bilirubin by photoisomerization and photo-oxidation
Bilirubin 4Z,15z -> 4z,15E which binds to albumin and is excreted by liver without conjugation
Photo-oxidation produces some polar bil. byproducts that are excreted in the urine
Goal is to prevent kernicterus
Difficult to predict, many guidelines for starting P/T have been suggested
Prophylactic
When rate of increase suggests bilirubin level will enter hazardous range
These curves were developed for infants with erythroblastosis and may be overly conservative curves
When clinical circumstances would suggest that newborn will have rapidly increasing bilirubin (eg. severely bruised premature infant)
Fluid management during phototherapy (黃疸處置建議手冊1st  p.32 )
照光身體須暴露，加上光照射會改變環境溫度、影響末梢循環，因此須注意嬰兒體溫和insensible water loss，如每日BW體重變化、尿量等，避免脫水現象。在沒脫水的情況給予額外的體液補充並無證據顯示有好處。
General observations
Light sources include blue light fluorescent lamps 45 cm above infants and phototherapy blankets
Infants should have minimal clothing
Eye patches should be worn by infants at all times
Since insensible water losses may be increased (FT 40%; preterm 80 - 200%) fluid intake should be encouraged or increased and infant weights followed

path: GI / Gastroenterology / Exchange Transfusion (科共同快速鍵 BET) (Part 1)
content:
Indications
Correct anemia in infants severely affected by hemolytic process
Cord bilirubin > 4.5 mg/dl and cord Hb < 11 gm/dl
Bilirubin increasing over 1 mg/dl/hr despite phototherapy and hydration
Hb 11-13 mg/dl and bilirubin increasing >0.5 mg/dl/hr despite phototherapy and hydration
Bilirubin > 25 mg/dl or appears will exceed this level
Prevent or correct hyperbilirubinemia that has been associated with neurologic sequelae
Expected effects of exchange transfusion
Beneficial
Lowers bilirubin by nearly 50%
Rebound bilirubin level reaches 60% of pre-exchange values within 0.5 hr
Greater rebound may reflect
RBC sequestered in spleen and bone marrow
Hemolysis of transfused RBC
Detrimental
Citrate in CPD blood may cause hypocalcemia and hypomagnesemia
Observe EKG and treat if indicated
Citrate may cause acid - base abnormalities
May be metabolized to alkali by liver of healthy infants
May not be metabolized by livers of critically ill infants and thus contribute to acidosis
Follow blood pH and treat as indicated
Dextrose may cause rebound hypoglycemia
May occur 1 – 2 hours following exchange transfusion
Monitor serum glucose and treat if hypoglycemia develops
Blood banked blood > 24 hours may have increased potassium
Fresh blood is used for exchange transfusions
Additional potential problems
Hemodynamic instability
Use appropriate volumes for exchange
Monitor patient closely during exchange transfusion
Consider other metabolic causes for instability noted above
Hypothermia: use warmed blood; treat patient under radiant warming unit
Sepsis: use sterile technique; remove catheters when indicated
Graft vs. host reaction
Reduced by irradiating blood before use
Manifests by maculopapular rash with eosinophilia, leukopenia and thrombocytopenia
If occurs is usually transient and not associated with other signs of immunodeficiency
Bleeding: rare; deficient clotting factors and thrombocytopenia
Procedure
Type of blood used for exchange transfusion
In ABO hemolytic disease:
“O” packed RBC 2 U + AB FFP 2 U( or baby same type plasma), to achieve the desired Hct level (45% for full term, 50% for premature)
If whole blood will be used, fresh ”O” type whole blood (less than 3 days old) with the low anti-A and anti-B titer
ABO compatible Rh-negative cells are used, cross-matched with the mother’s serum is required
If no ABO or Rh incompatibility problem, e.g.: Mother and baby have same blood type, use baby same blood type whole blood
Double-volume exchange transfusion
The amount of blood needed: 2 x BW (kg) x (80~95)ml/kg
Normal blood volume in full-term infant: 80 ml/kg
Normal blood volume in preterm infant: 95 ml/kg
Aliquots usually used in neonatal exchange transfusion
Total duration for double – volume ET: 90-120 mins
Conjugated hyperbilirubinemia (direct hyperbilirubinemia)
Definition: direct bilirubin level > 1-2 mg/dl and is >  20% of the total serum bilirubin
Etiology:
Extrahepato-biliary (obstruction): biliary atresia, choledochal cyst ….

path: GI / Gastroenterology / Exchange Transfusion (科共同快速鍵 BET) (Part 2)
content:
Mixed - Hepatocellular: TORCH infection, TPN-related cholestasis, hepatitis, sepsis
Intrahepato-biliar: alagille syndrome, inspissated bile syndrome
Other: endocrine (hypothyroidism), GALD
Workup (開立前請和主治醫師討論:
CBC/DC, TBI/DBI, GOT/GPT, rGT, ALKP, liver function (Alb, PT/aPTT, glu..),  B/C, U/A, U/C, urine virus culture, CMV IgG/IgM, NBS, thyroid function, 
genetic test, Abdominal sonography
Tc99 DISIDA: 在檢查前三天要服用 luminal 5mg/kg/day * 3days
MRCP
Management – Operative cholangiography, resolution of primary problems
Medication of cholestasis: Seminars in Pediatrics Surgery 9(2):96-102. 2000 (May)
可使用於膽汁滯留症藥物如下，可視狀況單獨或合併使用
Phenobarbital: 5 mg/kg/day, PO BID×6 weeks
Actigall: 5 mg/kg/dose, PO TID indefinitely
Kanamycin: 7.5 mg/kg/dose, PO BID×2 weeks
Cefoperazone: 50 mg/kg/dose, IV Q12H×6-8 weeks
Polymyxin B: 100,000-200,000 units/kg/d (10-20 mg/kg/d) divided into 3 or 4 doses, Use IV form PO. Never give this dose IV.
Consider cycling steroids: 10 mg PO bid x 4 weeks, then taper over 2 weeks and discontinue or 10 mg PO bid for 3 days every 2 weeks for 2-4 months
Ursodeoxycholic acid (100mg/tab): 15-20 mg/kg/day, BID or TID
Silymarin(150mg/tab): 1/2# QD or 1# QOD

path: GI / Gastroenterology / Prolonged jaundice (快速鍵 prolong)
content:
延遲性黃疸：足月兒超過二星期、或是早產兒超過三星期仍有黃疸。
Tests for liver disease, congenital infections, sepsis, metabolic defects, or hypothyroidism are indicate
Initial workup: CBC/DC, B/C, TBI/DBI, GOT/GPT, rGT, ALKP, U/A, U/C, urine virus culture, (CMV IgG/IgM), Abdominal sonography
If direct bilirubin is elevated, obtain urinalysis and urine culture, check state newborn screen for hypothyroidism and galactosemia, and check urine for reducing substances
開立醫囑前請與fellow 或主治醫師討論
Ref: Manual of Neonatal Care 8th ed, p. 343

簡介：原為放射科顯影劑由Noblett於1969年首先使用，主要利用其高滲透壓所造成鄰近器官釋放大量液體流入腸道後將堅硬胎便溶解攜出
Indication
Uncomplicated meconium ileus
Meconium plug syndrome
Complicated meconium ileus, meconium peritonitis post–operation, for removal of meconium
材料：顯影劑，可向CT室索取
目前CT室常備顯影劑為Omnipaque (Osmolarity 465mOsm/L), Ultravist (428mOsm/L),已不再常備Gastrografin (2150mOsm/L) or Urograffin ; 或者是用太陽牌人生浣腸 (15% glycerol + 15% NaCl, Osm約6000 mOsm/L)
方法
Dose: 將contrast與 N/S 以1: 1-4的比例稀釋後，取10-20ml/kg進行enema
Frequency: q12-24hours
Duration: <2 weeks
Intravenous fluids: before enema –L.R 20ml/kg over 30mins, after enema -1.5times maintenance
Meconium pellets followed by loose stool meconium pass over 4-8 hours in 4-14 days
若大於兩週仍無法將胎便沖出，則考慮手術治療。
若初始灌出的大便沒有膽汁染色，則需考慮可能為需要開刀之腸阻塞 
(許醫師寶貴經驗)
Complications: fluid depletion causes perforation, NEC, shock
Consider orally administered contrast: 3~7cc/dose (與N/S 1:1 dilute)
PO Gastrografin : water 1:2 稀釋， osmolarity 2150→717mOsm/L; 因Ultravist的Osmlarity(428)已經低了許多，所以不用多倍稀釋 
Ref: Haiden et al. 2012 Pediatrics
Stoma care (2021增)
Background information: 
A stoma is formed surgically by opening part of the bowel to the outside of the abdomen for the output of feces
Indication examples:
Necrotizing enterocolitis
Hirschsprung’s disease
High type imperforate anus
Complex intestinal malformation or volvulus
Intestinal atresia
Monitoring
每日體重變化
Total intake/output
Stoma losses, 顏色性狀也很重要
Serum electrolytes: hypokalemia and hyponatremia are common at initial stage
Urine sodium and potassium: urine electrolytes could serve as indicators of adequate electrolytes intake
Adequate sodium intake will give a urinary sodium concentration of ≥ 20 mmol/L
Nutritional considerations during stoma care
注意feeding intolerance
Vomiting, abdominal distension, bile in NG aspirates, large NG losses
Low stoma losses – obstruction or dysmotility
High stoma losses – malabsorption
Reducing substances or fat globules in the stoma effluent
理論上，stoma位置越遠，腸胃道耐受性越好
If breast milk is not available, consider using hydrolyzed formula
High stoma output:
Daily output ≥ 20ml/kg/day in premature or LBW babies and ≥30ml/kg/day in term babies
Replace excessive losses分班補量：實際操作上可以設每班loss超過某個標準，即開始補半量或全量
Replacement fluid: 依流失體液種類及量做考量。
舉例來說，若為ileostomy，流失液體鈉含量約45-135mEq/L，可以先以0.45% glucose saline補量，並監測電解質來決定後續補量點滴種類，欲減少鈉含量可改成0.33% glucose saline，欲增加可改成lactated ringer’s等等
若長期high output stome合併體重增加不足，可與主治醫師討論是否將部分補量點滴改成PN，但必須嚴格監測血糖、electrolytes等，避免過度波動，且需避免快速大量輸液
Ref: Morris et al. 2019 revised Wales Neonatal Network Guideline

GER drugs :
Metoclopramide (Primperan), 0.1ml/kg/dose
Domperidone (Motilium), 0.1ml/kg/dose
Action : Both central and peripheral DA-antagonist effects, blockade of DA-receptors and modulation of Ach release, ↑ lower esophageal pressure enhances gastric emptying.
Adverse effects : Irritability, somnolence, EPS (dystonias, akathisia, drug-induced Parkinsonism) — dose related, early in the initiation of therapy, reversible, GI symptoms (nausea, vomiting, diarrhea), urticaria, increased lactation, gynecomastia, fluid retention secondary to aldosterone release, methemoglobinemia
Bethanechol (25mg/tab) 1# in diswater 25mL=1mg/ml, 冰箱保存3-5天，0.1ml/kg/dose
多AC吃, UpToDate: max 4次/day,日本有paper 0.25mg/kg/dose Q3H AC
Action (for GER) : Acetylcholine receptor agonist, Increase lower esophageal pressure, increase the amplitude and duration of esophageal peristaltic contractions
Adverse effects: abdominal cramping, blurred vision, increased urinary frequency, dystonic reactions, wheezing, increased salivary and bronchial secretions, bronchospasm
Mosapride (5mg/tab)(off-label use in neonate,使用前須向家屬說明可能side effect)
[GI用法0.3mg/kg/day，科內用法0.6mg/kg/day，range 0.5-1mg/kg/day TIDAC]
Action: Diffuse effects along the entire GI tract, Enhances Ach release from postganglionic nerve endings of the myenteric plexus (increase antral motility and duodenal contractility) In premature infants: decrease gastric residuals, increase feeding volume, decrease vomiting, increase weight gain
Adverse effects : QT prolongation, torsades de pointes, diarrhea
Contraindicated in patients receiving macrolide antibiotics (erythromycin, clarithromycin), azole antifungals (fluconazole, ketoconazole, itraconazole), protease inhibitors (sequinavir , indinavir, ritonavir), class IA and III antiarrhythmics (quinidine, procanamide), astemizole
Erythromycin (目前無solution 。250mg/tab，不可磨粉-遇胃酸會失效)
類似Motilium 的效果，不可與cisapride 併用
Dose：低/中/高劑量分別為2.5/5/10~12.5 mg/kg/dose Q6H
H2-receptor antagonists :
Famotidine (Gaster)： (PO 20mg/tab，IV 20mg/2ml)
0.5 mg/kg/day QD，Max. dose for infant/children:1mg/kg/dose Q12H
PO: 1# in distilled water 10cc=2mg/ml，放冰箱存放24hr，膜衣錠可磨粉
Adverse effects : rash, dizziness, impaired liver function, agranulocytosis, leukopenia, thrombocytopenia
PPI
Nexium (Esomeprazole): PO/IVD 0.5mg/kg/day QD 
IVD: in N/S, 濃度 0.4-0.8mg/ml, drip for 30 min，與PN不相容

Neonatal hemogram normal range

Definition: 
Hb (central venous blood)＜13 g/dl or 
Hb (capillary venous blood)＜14.5 g/dl in neonates (＞GA 34 weeks)
Clinical presentations: Pallor, tachypnea, tachycardia, hypotension (hypovolemia with weak pulse), congestive heart failure, heart murmur, feeding problem
If anemia is chronic, patient may be asymptomatic other than pallor
If blood loss is acute, patient may be usually symptomatic

Obligatory initial studies
Hb
Red blood cell indices
Microcytic (MCV＜90fl) or hypochromic
Fetomaternal transfusion/hemorrhage
Twin-twin hemorrhage
α-thalassemia
Normocytic or normochromic
Acute hemorrhage
Systemic disease
Intrinsic red cell defect
Hypoplastic anemia
Reticulocyte count (corrected)
Corrected reticulocyte count＝
Blood smear
Spherocyte: ABO isoimmune hemolysis or hereditary spherocytosis
Elliptocyte: hereditary elliptocytosis
Schistocyte or helmet cell: consumptive coagulopathy
Direct Coombs’ test: isoimmune or autoimmune hemolysis
Other selected laboratory studies
Isoimmune hemolysis
Fetomaternal hemorrhage：Kleihauer-Betke test (Acid elution test) (抽媽媽的血)
2×2400×Ratio of fetal to maternal cells＝__ml of fetal blood lost into maternal circulation
Ex: 一位1kg的NB, 媽媽的Acid elution test為5/2000
代表baby有12 c.c.的血跑到媽媽身上 (2×2400×5/2000 = 12c.c.)
NB的全身血量為 : kg × 85 (Term)/ 95 (preterm)
這位NB的全身血量為95c.c.
NB blood loss量為 12/95 = 12.6%
In ABO incompatibility, which will result in increased clearance rate of fetal cells from the maternal circulation, giving a falsely low result
D.D. with maternal hemoglobinopathies with increased hemoglobin F levels, such as maternal thalassemia minor, sickle cell anemia, hereditary persistence of HbF and in some normal women, a pregnancy induced rise in HbF production
GI hemorrhage: Apt test （僅剛出生時GJ有血適合做）
Apt test (+) : HbF(+) →  infant GI bleeding
Apt test (−) : HbF(−) → 可能吞到媽媽的血
Congenital hypoplastic or aplastic anemia: bone marrow aspiration
TORCH infection
Skull and long bone films
IgM
Serological study
Urine culture for CMV (and/or urine CMV PCR, blood CMV viral load)
Consumption coagulopathy
PT and PTT
platelet count
TT or fibrinogen assay
FDP
fibrin
Occult hemorrhage: arrange brain and abdominal sonography
Intrinsic red blood cell defect

If patient is asymptomatic, observe and iron replacement (2-4 mg/kg) if necessary
Symptomatic patients require transfusion, draw initial blood samples (一定要用抽的，不可以採腳根血) before transfusion
Calculation for amount of blood to be transfused
3 mL of packed RBC/kg will increase Hb by 1gm
6 mL of whole blood/kg will increase Hb by 1gm
Infuse 15-20ml/kg pRBC，早產兒大部分都輸 20ml/kg，可間隔約24小時分兩次輸注，避免fluid overload，早產兒輸血標準參考Routine章節，一般新生兒輸血標準可參考下表 (Ref: Avery’s Diseases of the Newborn, 10th Ed, Table 81.7)
Choosing blood product
PRBC to avoid volume overload
Whole blood if acute blood loss of a large amount of blood
In newborn, use “O” P-RBC if there is possibility of ABO incompatibility 
(eg. mother “O” type, baby “A” or “B”)
Erythropoietin administration as prophylactic regimen
Partial Exchange transfusion for correction of severe anemia:
Volume(ml)= infant’s blood volume x (Hb desired- Hb initial)/(Hb of pRBC- Hb initial)
Packed RBC 的Hb大約18-26g/dL(Ht 55-80%，換算為Hb可除以3)，換血前請提前聯絡血庫幫忙驗
Infant’s blood volume in preterm infant: 90-95 ml/kg; in term infant: 80-85ml/kg

Polycythemia：venous Hct＞65%；warm heel stick Hct＞70%
Hyperviscosity
The relationship of Hct and viscosity is nearly linear below a Hct of 60%, but viscosity increases exponentially at a Hct of 70% or greater

Recipient of twin-twin transfusion
Maternal smoking
Other: potential causes: hypoxemia, placental dysfunction, hyperinsulinism or high maternal sugar level

Congestive heart failure
Respiratory distress
Seizure

path: Hematology / Hematology / Diagnosis and laboratory studies
content:
Central Hct level
Serum glucose level: hypoglycemia
Serum bilirubin level: hyperbilirubinemia
Serum Na and BUN: higher if dehydration is present
Urine specific gravity:＞1.015, indicating dehydration
ABG: evaluate oxygenation status
Serum platelet count: thrombocytopenia
Serum calcium level: hypocalcemia

Guidelines
Venous Hct＞70%  &  arterial Hct＞68%
With or without s/s→partial exchange transfusion (PET)
Venous Hct 65～70% &  arterial Hct 62～68%
With s/s→PET
Without s/s→  observation and avoid dehydration
Replacement agent in PET：N/S, FFP, 5% albumin, lactate Ringer’s solution
Replacement volume calculation：
Volume to exchanged＝BW (kg) ×BV×[Hct (o)-55%]/Hct (o)
Hct (o) : observed Hct
BV: blood volume for each kg of body weight
BV: 85ml/kg for term babies ,95ml/kg for every premature babies
55%: aim Hct for PET
eg. 3 kg infant, Hct 75%, BV＝80ML×3＝240 ml, aim Hct: 55%
Volume of exchange (ml)＝＝64ml exchange
Disorders of White Blood Cell  (2021 revised)

Definition of neutropenia: 
Neutropenia is defined as an absolute granulocyte count less than 1500 cells per ul. 
This may occur as a transient phenomenon associated with infection or as a chronic  process.

Infection or sepsis
Alloimmune neutropenia
Neutropenia associated with maternal disease
Severe congenital neutropenia (Kostmann syndrome)
Benign congenital neutropenia
Cyclic neutropenia
Neutropenia secondary to drugs

In association with other hematologic and/or physical examination abnormalities:
Bone marrow examination to rule out leukemia, neoplasm, or aplastic anemia
In the absence of other hematologic abnormalities:
Maternal neutrophil counts to rule out maternal disease or drugs
Neutrophil counts on family members to rule out hereditary neutropenia
Antineutrophil antibody determination to rule out immune neutropenia
Repeat neutrophil counts on baby (two times per week for 6 weeks) to rule out cyclic neutropenia
Bone marrow examination if associated with frequent bacterial infections to rule out agranulocytosis (Kostmann syndrome)

Treat underlying disease
G-CSF (Granulocyte-Colony Stimulating Factor) subcutaneous injection
Indications: neonatal sepsis with neutropenia, immune-mediated neutropenia, idiopathic neutropenia with ANC <500/μL
健保給付規定：先天性或循環性中性白血球低下症者(當白血球數量少於1000/μL，或中性白血球 (ANC) 少於500/μL)
Dose: 5-10 mcg/kg/day if necessary
Ref: Avery’s Diseases of the Newborn, 10th Ed, p.1189-1190

path: Hematology / Hematology / Hemorrhagic disease of newborn: Vit K deficiency
content:
The “healthy” infant is not given Vit K:
The infant may have been born in a busy delivery room, at home or transferred from elsewhere
＞48h/o: bleeding and bruising
Lab data: platelet normal; PT, PTT prolonged
As active bleeding: 10ml/kg FFP and 1mg Vit K IV
Maternal drug effect
Phenytoin (Dilantin), primidone (Mysoline), methsuximide (Celontin), or phenobarbital
During the first 24 hours
Management
The mother should be given Vit K 24 hours prior to delivery (10mg Vit K IM)
Vit K 1mg IM given as usual postpartum and repeated in 24 hours
If any bleeding occurs, FFP infusion
Delayed hemorrhagic disease of newborn
Malabsorption such as prolonged broad spectrum antibiotics or cystic fibrosis
4w/o～12w/o

Definition
Platelet count＜150,000/ml of blood in both term and premature infants
Platelet count nadir: 4 d/o , usually resolved: 10 d/o
General approach to diagnosis of thrombocytopenia
Maternal history：There may be a history of thrombocytopenia, bleeding before or during pregnancy, having undergone a splenectomy, drug use, or infection.
Infant：Healthy or sick, petechiae or large bruises, hepatosplenomegaly, jaundice, and congenital anomalies
Laboratory work-up
Mother: platelet count
Baby: CBC, platelet count, PT, PTT
Management
Platelet transfusion in the newborn, if necessary
indication: platelet＜20,000/ml
source: random donor, except in the infant with alloimmune thrombocytopenia
1U pl/3kg→↑50,000～100,000/ul
frequency: normal half life 4～5 days
route: never give platelet through an arterial line or into the liver, because thrombosis may occur
all platelet transfusion should be irradiated
Steroid therapy: prednisolone 2mg/kg/day
Emergency situation, fresh whole blood for exchange transfusion
IVIG: 1g/kg/day for 2 days for immune thrombocytopenia

Congenital factor deficiency－Factor replacement
Acquired coagulopathy: DIC (Disseminated Intravascular Coagulopathy)
Management
Treat underlying cause: e.g. sepsis, NEC
Vit K1 1mg IV
PLT and FFP transfusion to keep PLTt＞50,000/ul and to stop the bleeding
Treatment
In general, clinically ill infants or infant weighing less than 1500g are given the benefit of transfusion with FFP (10ml/kg) if the PT or PTT or both are greater than two times normal or with platelet if the platelet count is under 20,000/ul
NovoSeven – Recombinant factor VIIa use in neonates (2021年新增)
除了血友病以外，其他使用皆為off-label use
臨床應用：(皆為自費 NTD 28268/vial)
Life threatening bleeding which shows limited response to conventional therapy
Intolerability to conventional therapy
Acute, massive bleeding
Dose: 90-400mcg/kg/dose in case reports; repeat in 3-12 hours if needed
Complication: thrombotic events, especially in the presence of indwelling IV catheters
Ref: Journal of Perinatology (2006) 26, 706–713, J Perinatol. 2011 March ; 31(3): 188–192.

給前檢測PT,PTT, fibrinogen, anti-thrombin III, protein C,S (PTT管x 2)

每敷30分鐘休息15分鐘，交替一直到患部改善

path: Hematology / Hematology / Treatment of Thrombosis in the perinatal period
content:
Low-Molecular-Weight Heparin (LMWH) 1 mg=0.01ml
Advantages : minimal nonspecific binding to proteins, longer half-life, administered subcutaneously, less side-effect
Dosage
< 2 months of age : 1.5mg/kg/dose SQ Q12H
older children : 1.0mg/kg/dose SQ Q12H
Duration of therapy: varies from initial therapy alone to treatment for the entire 3-6 months

Natural course
Rapid growth within the first 3 months, 80% of final size in 5 months1
Slow proliferation during 6 to 12 months of age
Possible proliferation after the 1 year but uncommon
Presentation
Superficial: most common, bright red papule, nodule or plaque
Deep (subcutaneous): raised skin-colored nodule often with bluish hue, uncommon to present isolatedly
Combined: commonly presenting with superficial and deep contains
Evaluation
History: age of development, site, characteristics, numbers, previous treatment or response, symptoms of high-output heart failure (prolonged feeding or interrupted feeding, diaphoresis, irritability, failure to thrive)
PE: skin and mucous, morphology, location, size, ulceration or secondary infection, hepatosplenomegaly, signs of high-out heart failure (tachycardia, bounding pulse, mid-systolic murmur)
Special Consideration:
Beard distribution --> airway hemangioma, *PHACE syndrome
PHACE: Posterior fossa brain malformation, most commonly Dandy-Walker variant, Hemangioma (large or segmental), Arterial anomalies, Cardiac anomalies or CoA, Eye and Endocrine abnormalities
Multiple (≧5) cutaneous or large? hemangiomas with high-output heart failure→ hepatic hemangiomas
Lumbosacral distribution: spinal dysraphism or genitourinary anomalies
Differential diagnosis：Rapid growth with tenseness, purpuric appearance and thrombocytopenia: Kasabach-Merritt syndrome (important!!)
Work-up：Ultrasonography (especially abdomen), CT, MRI, Fiberoscopy (airway)
Management
Indications for treatment
Large, rapid progressing
Site: periorbital, airway, liver or GI tract, face?
Marked raise and sharp border or pedunculated lesions
Goal
Prevention or reversal of life-threatening complications
Minimizing the psychosocial distress for the patient and family
Adequate treatment of ulceration to avoid scarring, bleeding, infection and pain
Treatment
Local therapy: topical beta-blockers, intralesional corticosteroid

Indication:proliferating superficial infantile hemangioma
比較有公信力的paper引用的topical treatment多是用0.5% gel-forming solution，但是因為我們醫院只有眼藥水，因此以下都是用眼藥水的做法
Dose：3-15 drops 敷 15-30 min , BID~TID
Systemic therapy:

Mechanism (possible): vasoconstriction, decreased expression or vascular endothelial growth and basic fibroblast growth factor or triggering apoptosis
For infantile hemangiomas: Not approved in infants younger than 5 weeks
Dosing: 1-4 mg/kg/day, initially 0.5-1 mg/kg/day in 2-3 doses, increments of 0.5mg/kg/day in 1-2 weeks) with meals
Optimal maintenance dose, timing, and duration have not been established
Week 1: 0.6 mg/kg/dose orally twice daily, at least 9 hours apart
Week 2: 1.1 mg/kg/dose orally twice daily
Week 3: 1.7 mg/kg/dose orally twice daily for 6 months
Adjust the dose periodically as the child's weight increases. May re-initiate if hemangiomas recur. 2 to 3 mg/kg/day was recommended (guideline dosing)
Tapering periods have ranged from 2 weeks to 1 month
Dose adjustment: In infants with PHACE syndrome (posterior fossa defects, hemangiomas, cerebrovascular arterial anomalies, cardiovascular anomalies, and eye anomalies), especially in the presence of neurovascular anomalies, slowly titrate dose, use the lowest effective dose, and administer in 3 divided doses
Admission should be considered in the following situations:
≤8 weeks of corrected age
Infants of any age with inadequate social support
Infants of any age with comorbid conditions affecting the cardiovascular or respiratory system, including symptomatic airway hemangiomas
Infants of any age with conditions affecting blood glucose maintenance
Adverse effects: hypotension, bradycardia, bronchospasm (wheezing), hypoglycemia, hyperkalemia, anorexia
Ref. 1. NeoFax: Propranolol 2. (2019 AAP) Clinical Practice Guideline for the Management of Infantile Hemangiomas
Interferon-α, angiogenesis inhibitor: alternative therapy for aggressive hemangiomas not responsive to systemic corticosteroids
Dosing: 3 million units/m2/day
Duration: weeks to months
Adverse effects: fever, irritability, neutropenia, abnormal AST/ALT, neurotoxicity (spastic diplegia)
Vincristine
Indication: more aggressive hemangiomas as an alternative therapy for life-threatening or refractory to propranolol or corticosteroids
Adverse effects: peripheral neuropathy, constipation, jaw pain, leukopenia or anemia
Surgical therapies：Pulsed-dye laser, excisonal surgery, Embolization
註
